items.title: Always check status
items.avoid: Reading the body without checking res.ok.
items.prefer: Guard with res.ok and throw on errors.
items.why: Catches 4xx/5xx that fetch won't reject on.

items.title: Protect keys
items.avoid: Embedding API keys in browser code.
items.prefer: Call provider APIs from a backend/proxy.
items.why: Client keys are exposed to users.
items.example: Public Next.js page → `fetch('/api/chat', ...)` → server-side route reads `process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY` → calls OpenAI. Browser never sees the key in JS or network traffic.

items.title: Bound every call
items.example: Standardise a helper: `function fetchWithTimeout(url, opts, ms) { ... }` used across the codebase so no caller forgets to set a deadline.

items.title: Validate responses
items.prefer: Type as unknown and validate before use.
items.example:
```ts
const raw: unknown = await r.json()
const parsed = ApiResponse.parse(raw)   // zod / valibot — throws on shape mismatch
```
